overview: One shared UI design language for every XR Foundry system so a multi-contributor, multi-agent library reads as a single product rather than one visual style per package. Vision Pro is the primary visual reference; PICO and Meta Horizon OS are the primary interaction references. The language is expressed as renderer-neutral tokens, a state model, and content/interaction principles that each renderer adapter maps to; the renderer-neutral presentation contract carries no visual vocabulary.

interaction_primary.reference: PICO and Meta Horizon OS controller/ray interaction
interaction_primary.rationale: visionOS interaction is system-mediated gaze-and-pinch with no app-drawn pointer and does not transfer to controller/ray headsets; the target headset class uses a controller ray, so interaction facts come from PICO and Meta.

design_tokens.note: Canonical library target values that renderer adapters adopt so an un-themed install matches the shared look. These are XR Foundry's own chosen values, aligned with the spatial-glass palette of the Inventory UI Toolkit stylesheet; they are not copied from any platform, and they are the language's target, not a snapshot of any already-landed adapter. Colors are sRGB RGBA in 0..1.

panel.intent: translucent spatial glass; alpha < 1 keeps the environment visible

section.role: grouped region background (grid, details, action bar)

disabled.intent: dim the normal hue rather than recolor, so a disabled control reads as dimmed

shape.rounded_via: optional 9-slice sprite per surface; flat fill when no sprite is provided

elevation.technique: layered translucency plus soft shadow/highlight to signal depth, not heavy opaque borders

spacing.intent: generous separation so pointing at one interactive element does not crowd neighbors

hit_target_dp.small_visual_rule: extend to minimum with invisible hit-slop rather than enlarging the visual

motion.intent: subtle; avoid bright high-contrast motion in the periphery

rules: Every hover and selection event is confirmed on more than one channel.
The selected visual must reflect the underlying state: when the action a selection started (for example playback) stops or is toggled off, the selected visual clears in the same frame.
Disabled controls are dimmed, not recolored, and remain readable.

content_principles: No persistent instructional microcopy on repeated elements (for example a per-cell 'tap to preview' line). Affordance comes from shape, iconography, and interaction feedback, with any teaching kept to onboarding.

An icon that conveys an item's type replaces a redundant category text label, especially when category is already expressed by structure such as tabs or grouping. Category stays backend metadata, not chrome.
Labels are short and specific to the action; a view has one primary action paired with secondary or borderless actions.
interaction_principles: World-space UI is driven by a controller ray against a world-space canvas; controller trigger selects. Ray (distant) and direct/near (touch) interaction are separated so they do not fight over the same element.
Interactive targets are at least 48 dp, primary controls at least 60 dp, with hit-slop for smaller visuals; targets are not small or tightly packed.
The head ray is not used as the primary pointer.
Real-device verification is required before any comfort, precision, or usability claim; the language sets intent, not a device result.

principles: The renderer-neutral presentation contract carries no visual vocabulary; all look-and-feel lives in the renderer adapter.
Each renderer adapter exposes a single injectable skin/theme seam that maps these tokens, so a consumer can restyle without forking prefabs or code.
The adapter ships a default skin populated with the canonical token values above, so an un-themed install already matches the shared library look.
Interaction-state visuals follow the state model; content follows the content principles.

reference_implementation.current_state: The landed 0.2.0 adapter predates the injectable skin seam and does not yet map these tokens; it adopts the language through its own gate.

proposed_seam.seam: InventorySkin (ScriptableObject) injected via InventoryShellView.ApplySkin, propagated to panel/grid/slot/details/action-menu views
proposed_seam.visual_evidence: editor visual verification (rounded 9-slice sprite baking + State Gallery capture) tracked in https://github.com/Lingkyn/xr-foundry/issues/82

reference_implementation.note: The seam and 0.3.0 default palette are proposed in PR #81 (code-reviewed on that branch, blocked from merge by the editor evidence pass #82); they are not part of the currently landed 0.2.0 adapter. This standard defines the target the adapter converges to, not a completed implementation.

non_derivation: No platform HIG asset, shader, component, font, or numeric spec is copied; platform sources inform neutral roles and intent only.
Vision Pro visual admission does not import Vision Pro interaction; controller/ray interaction comes from PICO and Meta.
This standard does not mandate rebuilding any existing package UI; adoption is per family through its own gate.
No device usability, comfort, or accessibility result transfers across a renderer, runtime, device, or composition boundary.
